key_topics: Ratios and proportions: writing and solving proportions
Unit rates and dimensional analysis
Percent: percent of a number, discount, tax, tip, simple interest
Integers: all four operations with positive and negative numbers
Absolute value
Rational numbers: fractions and decimals on the number line
Algebra: writing and solving one-step and two-step equations
Writing and graphing inequalities
Variables and expressions: evaluating with substitution
Geometry: area of triangles, parallelograms, trapezoids
Surface area and volume of prisms
Coordinate plane: all quadrants, distances between points
Statistics: mean, median, mode, range, dot plots, box plots
competences: Solve percent problems: discount, tax, simple interest
Perform all operations with integers confidently
Write and solve two-step algebraic equations
Calculate surface area and volume of rectangular and triangular prisms
Create and interpret box plots and dot plots
typical_mistakes: Integer subtraction errors (subtracting a negative adds)
Not distributing correctly in algebraic expressions

key_topics: Reading: world literature — novels, short stories, poetry from global cultures
Comprehension: theme, symbol, irony, allegory
Literary criticism: introduction to different critical approaches
Informational text: evaluating sources for bias and credibility
Writing: analytical essays about literature
Persuasive essays: claim, warrant, evidence (Toulmin model)
Journalism: news writing, editorial writing
Grammar: complex and compound-complex sentences
Punctuation: all marks including semi-colons and dashes
Vocabulary: morphology, etymology, academic vocabulary (Tier 2)
Speech: persuasive speech, debate (Oxford style)
competences: Write analytical essays about literary themes and symbols
Evaluate the credibility and bias of informational sources
Write persuasive essays using claim-warrant-evidence structure
typical_mistakes: Using only one source for research
Confusing complex and compound sentences in grammar
Not varying sentence length in essays

key_topics: Ancient World: Mesopotamia, Egypt, Persia — comparative study
Ancient Greece: philosophy (Socrates, Plato, Aristotle), history
Ancient Rome: Republic to Empire, Augustus, Pax Romana
World Religions: Judaism, Christianity, Islam — origins and core beliefs
Medieval Europe: feudalism, Black Death, Renaissance
Byzantine Empire and Islamic Golden Age
World Geography: Africa — cultures, resources, colonialism
Historical thinking skills: cause-effect, continuity-change, perspective
competences: Compare and contrast major ancient civilisations
Explain the origins and core beliefs of the three Abrahamic religions
Describe the Byzantine Empire's role in preserving classical knowledge
Apply historical thinking skills to primary sources
typical_mistakes: Confusing Greek and Roman political history
Oversimplifying causes of the fall of Rome

key_topics: Life Science: plant biology — photosynthesis, transpiration, tropisms
Animal biology: circulatory, respiratory, digestive systems comparison
Ecology: ecosystems, trophic levels, biomes
Earth Science: oceanography — ocean features, currents, tides
Meteorology: cloud types, weather patterns, severe weather
Physical Science: matter — periodic table, atomic structure basics
Chemical reactions: conservation of mass, types of reactions
Physics: energy — mechanical, thermal, electrical, light
competences: Explain photosynthesis and transpiration in plants
Describe major ocean features and their global impact
Classify organisms by trophic level in an ecosystem
typical_mistakes: Thinking the ocean floor is flat
